subject: Databases
title: TSB Bank Migration Disaster
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on TSB Bank Migration Disaster.

hook.text: April 2018. TSB migrates 5.4 million customers from one banking platform to another. It goes catastrophically wrong.

buildup.text: Customers see other people's accounts. Payments fail. Mobile and online banking break. 1.9 billion records to migrate.

discovery.text: The migration was rushed. Testing was inadequate. The new platform wasn't ready for production load.

twist.text: Outage lasted weeks, not hours. TSB lost 80,000 customers and the CEO resigned. The FCA fined them £48.65 million.

climax.text: Lesson: migrations this large need months of parallel running. Don't switch until the new system proves itself.

punchline.text: Big-bang migrations are high risk. Run systems in parallel. Migrate gradually.

quiz.question: What was the main cause of the TSB migration disaster?
options: The migration was rushed with inadequate testing, and the new platform wasn't ready for production load
A natural disaster destroyed the new data center
Customers refused to use the new system
